name: Gas exchange half-saturation surface area
tier_rationale: Tier C: morphometric model parameter from Mayhew 1986 Placenta (PMC-verified): the term-placenta morphometric diffusing capacity depends most strongly on harmonic-mean membrane thickness and villous surface area. The half-saturation area is a model-fit abstraction, not directly reported.
notes: Villous surface area at which placental gas-equilibration efficiency reaches half of its asymptote. Caveats: Phenomenological model parameter mapping morphometric diffusing capacity onto a saturable equilibrator. Validate against UV PO2 endpoint.

name: Maximum venous-equilibrator fraction
tier_rationale: Tier C from Carter & Pijnenborg 2011 BPOBG: review of invasive placentation in primates including human. Maximum equilibration (fraction of P_arterial - P_venous that the placenta can clear) is a model abstraction informed by morphometric and Doppler-flow constraints.
notes: Asymptotic upper bound on placental gas-equilibration efficiency as villous area increases. Caveats: Saturation cap derived to reproduce the textbook 25-40 mmHg UV PO2 window at term.

name: Maternal intervillous-space PO2
tier_rationale: Tier B from Carter 2009 (PMID 19070361) review of placental O2 transfer: maternal intervillous-space PO2 at term ~50 mmHg, intermediate between maternal arterial (~100) and venous (~40) values due to mixing in the intervillous lake. Replicated across placental-physiology references.
notes: Lower than maternal arterial because intervillous blood is largely venous-mixed by the time of gas exchange.

name: Umbilical-vein PO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Carter 2009 (PMID 19070361): umbilical-vein PO2 (the most oxygenated fetal blood, returning from placenta to fetus) is ~30 mmHg at term — a striking contrast with adult post-pulmonary PO2 (~100 mmHg). The fetal cardiovascular architecture (DV bypass, foramen ovale, ductus arteriosus) is shaped around delivering this low-PO2 blood preferentially to brain and heart.

name: Umbilical-artery PO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Carter 2009 (PMID 19070361): umbilical-artery PO2 (de-oxygenated fetal blood returning to placenta) ~17 mmHg at term. The umbilical V-to-A PO2 gradient (~30 → 17) reflects fetal tissue O2 extraction over the systemic circulation.

name: Maternal-fetal PCO2 gradient at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Mayhew 1986 and standard fetal-physiology references: at term the umbilical-vein PCO2 (~40 mmHg) exceeds the intervillous PCO2 (~30 mmHg under maternal hyperventilation) by ~10 mmHg, while umbilical artery PCO2 runs ~50 mmHg. The CO2 gradient drives net CO2 transfer from fetus to mother, supported by the Haldane effect and the high CO2 diffusing capacity of the placental membrane. Tier C reflects substantial cohort variation.
notes: Maternal respiratory alkalosis (lower PaCO2) widens the gradient and facilitates fetal CO2 offloading — a teleologically attractive coupling between maternal and fetal acid-base physiology.

name: Placental CO2 diffusing capacity at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Mayhew 1986 stereology of placental gas-diffusing capacity: CO2 diffusing capacity is broadly comparable to O2 diffusing capacity (~6 mL/min/mmHg at term) given the dominant role of villous-membrane area and thickness, both of which are shared. Direct measurement is rare; the value is back-calculated from morphometry, so Tier C.
notes: CO2 transfers efficiently because of its higher water/lipid solubility — even with smaller maternal–fetal gradients than for O2, fluxes balance.

name: Umbilical vein PCO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Carter 2009 (placental gas exchange review summarising cord-blood gas data at delivery): umbilical-vein PCO2 sits ~10 mmHg above the maternal intervillous PCO2 because the placenta runs venous-equilibration limited for CO2. Replicated across cord-blood gas series at uncomplicated term deliveries.

applicability.population: Healthy singleton term deliveries, immediate cord-blood sampling

notes: UV PCO2 is the fetal-arterial-side equivalent of maternal venous PCO2; the gradient drives placental CO2 clearance.

name: Umbilical artery PCO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Carter 2009 (placental gas exchange review summarising cord-blood gas data at delivery): umbilical-artery PCO2 (~50 mmHg) reflects the deoxygenated, CO2-loaded blood returning from the fetus to the placenta. The ~10 mmHg gradient between UA and UV (40 mmHg) drives placental CO2 clearance. Replicated across modern obstetric cord-gas series.

applicability.population: Healthy singleton term deliveries, immediate cord-blood sampling

notes: Markedly elevated UA PCO2 with low UA pH at delivery is the classical pattern of intrapartum hypoxia.

name: Pre-intervillous (spiral-artery) PO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Carter & Pijnenborg 2011 placental gas-exchange review: spiral-artery PO2 entering the intervillous space is estimated indirectly (cannot be sampled in vivo) at ~70–95 mmHg, slightly below the maternal arterial PO2 because of mixing with venous-side blood at the decidual junction. The exact value is model-dependent.
notes: Failed spiral-artery remodelling in pre-eclampsia lowers this value substantially — but that pathology is out of scope for nidus.
